message:
feat(robot): add --robot-dashboard for AI orchestrator consumption

This commit adds a new robot mode output format designed for AI orchestrators
that need a comprehensive view of the fleet status in a structured format.

New DashboardOutput Struct (robot.go):
- GeneratedAt: UTC timestamp of output generation
- Fleet: Working directory name as fleet identifier
- Agents: List of sessions with their agent details
- Beads: BeadsSummary from bv package (ready/in-progress/blocked)
- Alerts: Active alerts with severity, message, and duration
- AlertSummary: Aggregate alert counts
- Metrics: Extensible key-value metrics map
- AgentMail: Agent Mail status summary
- Conflicts: File conflict detection results
- System: OS, architecture, Go version, NTM version info
- Summary: Aggregate counts by agent type
- FileChanges: Recent file change tracking

PrintDashboard() Function:
- Best-effort collection: failures don't block output generation
- Gathers session/agent data from tmux if available
- Aggregates summary counts by agent type (claude, codex, gemini, etc.)
- Fetches beads summary via bv.GetBeadsSummary()
- Collects active alerts via alerts.GetActiveAlerts()
- Includes conflict and file change data
- Outputs as JSON via encodeJSON()

StatusSummary Enhancement:
- Add Beads field to include bead pipeline state in summary

buildSnapshotAgentMail Signature Change:
- Now accepts sessions parameter to reuse already-fetched session data
- Avoids redundant tmux queries during snapshot generation

Helper Functions (helpers.go):
- mustGetwd(): Safe working directory getter with fallback to "."
- hostname(): Safe hostname getter that ignores errors

CLI Integration (root.go):
- Add --robot-dashboard flag (robotDashboard bool)
- Wire flag to PrintDashboard() call in root command
- Outputs JSON format suitable for parsing by AI agents

Use Case:
This output format enables meta-orchestrating agents to:
- Monitor fleet health across all sessions
- Track bead pipeline bottlenecks
- Respond to alerts programmatically
- Coordinate work assignment based on agent availability

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
